behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure appears at a handoff boundary between diff extraction and review-input construction, not just inside one pure function. INTEGRATION DUAL-QUOTE GATE: Unit-floor evidence: "review input is scoped to the lines that changed" shows boundary logic between changed-line selection and downstream review input. Ceiling-stop evidence: "not the full content of each modified file" defines a deterministic scoping invariant (data-shaping rule), not model-quality judgment or OS/network behavior. REAL-INFRA: no real HTTP/subprocess/OS event is required to catch this bug; a local in-process diff fixture is sufficient. LLM-DEP: correctness here is the scoped input set, not what a model decides. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text does not require a real PR/API call; Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text asserts scoping, not model decision quality or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the diff parser + input-builder modules with a synthetic patch containing both changed and unchanged hunks; assert the produced review payload includes only changed-line snippets and excludes unchanged file content.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: correct diff-position attachment depends on GitHub's real diff/position semantics and API behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked clients often accept invalid positions that the real API rejects or remaps; real failure is hidden without live HTTP. LLM-DEP: not about model judgment quality; it is API/position correctness. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior explicitly targets "the diff view on the pull request," which is an external system boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real test PR with known diff hunks, post inline comments using computed positions, then fetch PR review comments from GitHub and verify each returned comment is anchored to the intended file and diff location.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on model judgment (detecting a real bug), which can vary run-to-run and cannot be validated by deterministic stubs. MOCK-HIDE: a mocked model output would always pass and hide detection-quality failures. MULTI-STEP: this behavior is a single quality judgment target, not a required multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: Integration/System rejected because behavior text requires the agent to "identify" a "real defect," i.e., model output quality, not just infrastructure wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Use a benchmark PR containing a deliberately introduced bug and run live model analysis; pass if at least one finding correctly flags the known defect location/category within acceptance criteria.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when summary assembly consumes outputs from inline-comment posting (cross-module state handoff). INTEGRATION DUAL-QUOTE GATE: Unit-floor evidence: "corresponds to a posted inline comment ID" requires linking two components (comment poster -> summary builder). Ceiling-stop evidence: "from the same run" defines deterministic run-state consistency, not model-quality evaluation or required real OS/network effects. REAL-INFRA: this invariant can be caught with in-process wiring and fake post responses carrying IDs. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model judgment quality. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because real network is not required to detect ID-mapping mismatch; Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior checks referential integrity, not model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute orchestration with a fixed finding set and stubbed inline-post responses returning comment IDs; assert each summary entry references an existing ID emitted during that run and no foreign/missing IDs appear.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped from the final summary payload before posting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: drop bugs occur at module boundaries (collector/filter/serializer -> summary payload), not necessarily within one isolated function. INTEGRATION DUAL-QUOTE GATE: Unit-floor evidence: "inline finding" vs "final summary payload" implies handoff across components. Ceiling-stop evidence: "before posting" scopes this to pre-send payload completeness, a deterministic count/content invariant with no required model judgment. REAL-INFRA: can be caught without real HTTP by asserting payload just prior to post call. LLM-DEP: independent of model output quality when using fixed findings. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because the failure is observable pre-network; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no model decision sequence is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline with a fixed inline-finding list through transformation to summary payload (post call stubbed) and assert one-to-one preservation of findings (no losses, no accidental filtering).

behaviors.description: The automation sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) succeeds for a real pull request when provided a fixed precomputed finding set.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly requires "real pull request" and real sequence execution including posting comments; this crosses HTTP/API boundaries. MOCK-HIDE: fakes can miss auth, permission, rate-limit, payload-shape, and endpoint-order failures. LLM-DEP: findings are precomputed/fixed, so model judgment is not under test. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected due explicit "real pull request" requirement; Agentic rejected by "fixed precomputed finding set" which disqualifies model-quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Against a live test PR, feed a fixed finding set, execute read-diff -> inline-post -> summary-post, and verify API success plus expected artifacts (inline comments and top-level summary) are present on the PR.

behaviors.description: The full live run with model-generated findings (read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary) completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP: this is an end-to-end agent pipeline with sequenced stages and at least one real model decision governing downstream actions. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on model-generated findings, not just transport plumbing. REAL-INFRA: also uses a real PR/API, but the dominant minimum boundary is multi-step agentic orchestration. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected as too low because behavior text explicitly requires full sequence "read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary" and "without manual intervention," i.e., pipeline-level autonomy across steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run the complete live automation on a real test PR with no human edits mid-run; assert it autonomously performs analysis, posts inline comments derived from generated findings, and posts a consistent top-level summary referencing those posted comments.
